title: Webhooks, Replay Defense, SSRF, Unsafe Consumption, and Business Abuse · Contract Lens: Business Flow Abuse
summary: A gateway parses before signature verification, accepts stale duplicates, follows arbitrary callback URLs, trusts upstream JSON, and protects expensive workflows only with request counts. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts business flow abuse increment in signed ingress, bounded egress, and abuse decision model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model automation-sensitive business operations with actor, cost, inventory, velocity, sequence, idempotency, anomaly, friction, appeal, and recovery evidence.
Authenticate upstreams, bound redirects, time and bytes, validate response type and schema, sanitize content, minimize authority, and contain upstream failure.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Business Flow Abuse · Theory Business Flow Abuse
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 7281 in a privacy-sensitive records API using conditional requests. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine signed ingress freshness, replay defense, and SSRF egress policy; keep rollback safe during a storage expansion; then make OpenAPI and runtime disagree on nullability and block the release. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Business Flow Abuse contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Node and Express server Predict Business Flow Abuse · Theory Business Flow Abuse.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for model automation-sensitive business operations with actor, cost, inventory, velocity, sequence, idempotency, anomaly, friction, appeal, and recovery evidence. For Node and Express server Predict Business Flow Abuse · Theory Business Flow Abuse, keep Business Flow Abuse connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.text: Name runtime, method, authority, route, unknown-input boundary, identity, resource budgets, middleware and dependency ownership, response effect, cleanup, and recovery; run a pure deterministic TypeScript decision model; then change one input or failure and state the Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, or production transfer gate. Apply that move specifically to Business Flow Abuse while producing signed ingress, bounded egress, and abuse decision model.
